top.title: US and Iran launch new strikes, as Kuwait says airport hit by Iranian drones
top.summary: Kuwait says one person is dead after drones hit civilian buildings including its international airport.

top.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon but partial truce with Hezbollah appears to hold
top.summary: Israel continues its campaign in the south, though has not struck Beirut after a deal announced by the US.

top.title: Seven killed after drone hits bus in Russia-controlled part of Ukraine
top.summary: The bus was travelling between Moscow and Simferopol, in Russian-occupied Crimea, an official says.

top.title: Meta won't track its workers' clicks - but only for half an hour at a time
top.summary: According to an internal memo, new controls will allow employees to pause the data collection for "up to 30 minutes at a time".

top.title: Publishers in UK can opt out of Google AI search results
top.summary: The Competition and Markets Authority says it would put publishers "in a stronger position to negotiate content deals with Google".

top.title: Do you find yourself aimlessly scrolling? You're not alone
top.summary: A Virgin Media O2 report suggests an average of 36% of time spent on phones is without a clear purpose.

title.#text: Trans teens have something to say
AI has a water problem. Google thinks it has a fix
Google must let publishers opt out of AI Search features, rules UK
